solution: 
```cpp
#include <vector>
#include <unordered_set>
#include <unordered_map>
#include <cmath>
#include <algorithm>

using namespace std;

class Solution {
public:
    vector<int> maximumCount(vector<int>& nums, vector<vector<int>>& queries) {
        int max_num = 1e5 + 1;
        vector<int> is_prime(max_num, 0);
        vector<int> prefix_sum(max_num, 0);
        vector<int> suffix_sum(max_num, 0);
        vector<int> prime_count(max_num, 0);
        vector<int> result;

        // Sieve of Eratosthenes to find primes
        for (int i = 2; i < max_num; ++i) {
            if (is_prime[i] == 0) {
                for (int j = i; j < max_num; j += i) {
                    is_prime[j] = 1;
                }
            }
        }

        // Count distinct primes in nums
        unordered_set<int> primes_in_nums;
        for (int num : nums) {
            if (is_prime[num]) {
                primes_in_nums.insert(num);
            }
        }

        // Map each prime to its positions
        unordered_map<int, vector<int>> prime_positions;
        for (int i = 0; i < nums.size(); ++i) {
            if (is_prime[nums[i]]) {
                prime_positions[nums[i]].push_back(i);
            }
        }

        // Build prefix and suffix sums of distinct primes
        vector<int> prefix(nums.size(), 0);
        vector<int> suffix(nums.size(), 0);
        unordered_set<int> prefix_primes;
        for (int i = 0; i < nums.size(); ++i) {
            if (is_prime[nums[i]] && prefix_primes.find(nums[i]) == prefix_primes.end()) {
                prefix_primes.insert(nums[i]);
            }
            prefix[i] = prefix_primes.size();
        }

        unordered_set<int> suffix_primes;
        for (int i = nums.size() - 1; i >= 0; --i) {
            if (is_prime[nums[i]] && suffix_primes.find(nums[i]) == suffix_primes.end()) {
                suffix_primes.insert(nums[i]);
            }
            suffix[i] = suffix_primes.size();
        }

        // Process each query
        for (auto& q : queries) {
            int idx = q[0];
            int val = q[1];
            nums[idx] = val;

            // Update prime_positions and prefix/suffix arrays
            if (is_prime[nums[idx]]) {
                prime_positions[nums[idx]].push_back(idx);
            } else {
                prime_positions[nums[idx]].clear();
            }

            // Rebuild prefix and suffix arrays
            prefix_primes.clear();
            for (int i = 0; i < nums.size(); ++i) {
                if (is_prime[nums[i]] && prefix_primes.find(nums[i]) == prefix_primes.end()) {
                    prefix_primes.insert(nums[i]);
                }
                prefix[i] = prefix_primes.size();
            }

            suffix_primes.clear();
            for (int i = nums.size() - 1; i >= 0; --i) {
                if (is_prime[nums[i]] && suffix_primes.find(nums[i]) == suffix_primes.end()) {
                    suffix_primes.insert(nums[i]);
                }
                suffix[i] = suffix_primes.size();
            }

            int max_sum = 0;
            for (int k = 1; k < nums.size(); ++k) {
                max_sum = max(max_sum, prefix[k - 1] + suffix[k]);
            }
            result.push_back(max_sum);
        }

        return result;
    }
};
```
efficiency: MEDIUM
time complexity: O(N log log N + Q * N)
space complexity: O(N + P), where P is the number of primes up to 10^5
energy implications: MEDIUM
explanation: The solution uses the Sieve of Eratosthenes to precompute prime numbers up to 10^5. For each query, it updates the array and rebuilds prefix and suffix arrays that track the count of distinct prime numbers up to each index. The maximum sum of distinct prime counts is computed for all possible splits after each query. The main strength is the efficient prime detection, but the weakness is the O(N) time complexity for each query due to rebuilding prefix and suffix arrays.
